title.en: CYCLICALLY OPERATING VALVES FOR MACHINES OR ENGINES

Valve-gear or valve arrangements, e.g. lift-valve gear (lift-valve and valve-seat assemblies per se F01L3/00; slide-valve gear F01L5/00; actuated non-mechanically F01L9/00; valve arrangements in working piston or piston rod F01L11/00; modifications of valve-gear to facilitate reversing, braking, starting, changing compression ratio, or other specific operations F01L13/00)
Valve drive (transmitting-gear between valve drive and valve F01L1/12)
Transmitting gear between valve drive and valve (simultaneously operating two or more valves F01L1/26)
Adjusting or compensating clearance

characterised by the provision of two or more valves operated simultaneously by same transmitting-gear; peculiar to machines or engines with more than two lift-valves per cylinder (with coaxial valves F01L1/28)
characterised by the provision of coaxial valves; characterised by the provision of valves co-operating with both intake and exhaust ports
characterised by the provision of positively opened and closed valves, i.e. desmodromic valves
characterised by the provision of means for rotating lift valves, e.g. to diminish wear
characterised by the provision of means for changing the timing of the valves without changing the duration of opening {and without affecting the magnitude of the valve lift}
peculiar to machines or engines of specific type other than four-stroke cycle
Multiple-valve gear or arrangements, not provided for in preceding subgroups, e.g. with lift and different valves

with valves other than cylindrical, sleeve, or part-annularly-shaped, e.g. flat D-valves
